title: Israel
html:
Israel

The middle leg of the September 2023 Jordan/Israel/Egypt arc — Yahir's substantive Mid-East trip, traveled with Emilio (per the 2024 08 14 travel occurrences Petra entry implying brother-companion). Israel sits between Jordan (the Petra near-miss) and Egypt (the white desert food poisoning) in the trip's geographic and narrative spine. The only Levant country where the trip log records thick urban tourism rather than wilderness mishaps.

The May 2023 trip-log 2023 05 13 travel trip log is the cryptic preview — "Jordan/Israel/Egypt - refer to journal entry" — meaning Yahir is treating the Mid-East stretch as substantial enough to warrant a separate long-form note rather than a bullet list. The actual trip log lives in 2023 09 06 egypt jordan itinerary (September 2023, real-time entries from on the ground), where the Jerusalem itinerary is the densest single-city plan in the entire Mid-East trip:

  • Old City via Jaffa Gate
  • Church of the Holy Sepulchre
  • Dome of the Rock
  • Garden Tomb
  • Yad Vashem (the Holocaust memorial)
  • Machane Yehuda market
  • Gethsemane Garden
  • Tomb of the Virgin Mary

That itinerary is eight stops, half religious-historical and half cultural, suggesting Yahir was treating Jerusalem as the trip's intellectual center of gravity rather than just a tourist box-tick. Yad Vashem is the heaviest item on the list — a deliberate engagement with the Holocaust memorial — and its proximity to the Christian and Muslim holy sites in the same itinerary tells you that Yahir was running the comparative-religion experience that Jerusalem uniquely enables. The 2024 2024 07 04 questions mega notebook later carries the religious-tradition cheat-sheet ("Christian — love thy neighbor / turn cheek; Islam — max effort full commit, devotion; Judaism — duty to provide for family; Hindu — life-as-illusion/game"), which reads as the synthesized output of that comparative-religion exposure beginning here.

The transit-arc story comes from 2024 08 14 travel occurrences: "Essentially moneyless between Jordan and Israel thanks to a rogue credit union." The Levant border-crossing produced the trip's hardest financial moment — Yahir's US credit union froze his card mid-trip, leaving him cash-poor across borders. The fact that this single mishap survives three years later in the story-bank tells you it was the trip's defining FITFO moment.

Israel matters in the broader graph as the religious-historical anchor of the Mid-East cluster, complementing Jordan's wilderness register and Egypt's food/desert register. Together with India, Vietnam, and the rest of the pre-China chapter geography, Israel is part of the three-continent travel arc Yahir had completed by the time he landed in Shanghai in September 2024.
